name: blob_centroid_detector
description: Find dark circular blobs on a noisy gradient background and emit centroids as a JSON array. One pass: gaussian blur, local-mean background subtract, threshold, morph close, connected components, area filter, centroid mean.

params.name: blur_radius
params.doc: Gaussian denoise radius applied before background subtract. 2 is plenty for sigma=8 noise.

params.name: block_radius
params.doc: Box-blur radius used to estimate the local-mean background. Must be larger than the blob radius (~14) so the blob does not pull its own background dark.

params.name: diff_C
params.doc: How much darker than local background a pixel must be to enter the mask. Tune up if the noisy bg leaks into the mask, down if blob edges are fragmenting.

params.name: close_radius
params.doc: Morphological close radius applied to the mask before labelling. 1 seals noise pinholes inside the disc without merging neighbours.

params.name: min_area
params.doc: Areas below this are dropped as noise (rejected_small). r=14 disc is ~615 px — 200 gives 3x margin.

params.name: max_area
params.doc: Areas above this are dropped as merged blobs / artefacts (rejected_big).

outputs.name: cleaned
outputs.doc: Mask after morphological close — what's actually labelled.

outputs.name: count
outputs.doc: Number of regions accepted by the area filter (= number of centroids).

outputs.name: total_regions
outputs.doc: Connected components found before area filtering.

outputs.name: centroids
outputs.doc: Array of {x:int,y:int,area:int} — centroid of each accepted region.
